instNote:
﻿This institution consists of three distinct branches, having the same end, nearly the same rules, the same vows of obedience, chastity and poverty, the same interests, and the same government, viz: 1. The "Priests of the Holy Cross," consecrated to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. Their constitutions are similar to those of St. Ignatius of Loyola; they devote themselves to missions and to education, in which they are assisted by the Brothers. 14 professed and 6 novices.
2d. The Brothers—similar to the Christian Brothers—consecrated to the Most Holy Heart of St. Joseph, and chiefly instituted to teach the poor and the destitute; they take charge of orphan asylums and teach useful trades; they also discharge the manual offices connected with a college and a farm. The most suitable age for admission is from fifteen to thirty-five, and the qualifications are chiefly a blameless character, sound constitution, a true piety, pure morals, a firm and conciliating temper, and a great desire of personal sanctification in the line of obedience. When the Brothers are sent out they must be two at least, and they receive a salary of $100 per annum, unless they take the school on their own account. Their novitiate is of one year, and they cannot make their perpetual vows before they are 25 years of age. Number of professed, 25; novices, 12; postualants, 9; number of boys in the manual labor school, 30.
3d. The "Sisters of the Holy Cross," consecrated to the most Immaculate Heart of Mary, like the Sisters of Charity, are devoted to the education of youth, to the service of orphan asylums and hospitals; they also discharge, for the colleges of the institute, the various functions of infirmarians, sacristans, &c. Their novitiate is at Bertrand, Berrien county, Michigan, five miles from Notre-Dame. They have also there an academy and a female orphan asylum, nearly on the same plan as that of the Brothers. Number of professed sisters, 23; novices, 12; postulants, 18. To be admitted either into the male or female orphan asylum, children must be at least twelve years old, and they are bound to remain until they are 21 years of age. They must pay $40 as security, and this sum is returned in clothes at the expiration of their time.
